Upload revised artifacts and re-run grader/verifier.

planned_changes.details: In sheet '02_Check_Expressions' create named cells status_plus_10pct and status_minus_10pct for each controlling threshold. Replace existing +/-5% logic with +/-10% and ensure these sensitivity cells feed into final classification formulas (PASS/WARN/FAIL/needs_review).

In '01_Packet_Raw' add a column 'Row_ID' with unique identifiers (R1, R2, ...) for each packet row. Update all intermediate and final formulas across sheets to reference those Row_ID cells (e.g., 01_Packet_Raw!R3) rather than only dotted field names.
Audit workbook to remove hard-coded numeric overrides. Convert all stepwise calculations and decision aggregations into spreadsheet formulas so the workbook is executable and authoritative.

planned_changes.description: Link sensitivity cells to classification outputs
planned_changes.details: Make the classification cell for each metric evaluate status based on nominal value and the +/-10% sensitivity checks so that metrics within +/-10% produce a 'WARN' or 'needs_review' designation per rubric.

Run an automated parse using openpyxl (or equivalent) to confirm formulas and references are present. Save a short parsing log (list of sheets, key formula cells, and their formulas) and include it as an additional artifact (validation_log.txt).

Revise memo to (a) cite explicit raw-packet Row_IDs for each evidence line, (b) document the +/-10% sensitivity behavior and how borderline cases are categorized, (c) list any packet fields intentionally excluded, and (d) include a short summary of the parsing/validation log or attach it as an appendix.

After making changes, re-run the CI/validation that uses openpyxl and include outputs (validation_log.txt, optionally CSV exports of key sheets) so the grader/verifier can programmatically confirm the updates.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Address sensitivity magnitude requirement, improve traceability to raw-packet rows, and ensure workbook formulas are parseable and linked to classification outputs so automated verification can confirm semantics.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables are present and provide traceable packet-field citations and a clear recommended mode (single_tracker). Primary shortcomings: (1) sensitivity checks are implemented at +/-5% but the rubric requires +/-10%, leaving borderline handling incomplete; (2) the memo uses dotted embedded_packet field identifiers but does not include explicit raw-packet row identifiers (e.g., R1, R2) for cell-level traceability; (3) the workbook could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so live formulas, explicit cell references, and linked sensitivity cells could not be independently verified. Address these to meet full rubric expectations and enable automated verification.
